engineIntelligence.h2: Chevrolet Equinox Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chevrolet Equinox in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Equinox LEA engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Excessive oil consumption - piston ring wear causes burning from 60k miles.
Timing chain tensioner failure - leads to rattling noise and potential engine damage.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Equinox LYX engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking - causes boost irregularities and check engine light.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - affects direct injection performance over time.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Equinox LH7 engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey use - causes limp mode and reduced performance.
EGR valve carbon buildup - leads to rough idle and emissions faults.

engineGuide.h2: Chevrolet Equinox Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chevrolet Equinox across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: The workhorse 2.4L Ecotec powered most early Equinox models, known for decent refinement but oil consumption issues.
commonFailures: Excessive oil consumption due to piston ring wear, typically from 60k-100k miles
Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling noise and potential engine damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for LEA Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Updated version of the 2.4L Ecotec with minor revisions, still prone to oil consumption in high-mileage examples.
commonFailures: Oil consumption from piston ring design, requiring top-ups between services
Timing chain stretch on neglected units leading to performance loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for LAF Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: Modern direct-injection turbo replaced the 2.4L, offering better efficiency but introducing turbo-specific failure points.
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking causing boost irregularities and check engine light
Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance on direct injection units
entries.cta: Get quotes for LYX Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: Revised version of the 1.5L turbo with incremental reliability improvements for late-model Equinox.
commonFailures: Early production units may exhibit turbo lag from wastegate calibration
Carbon deposits on intake valves requiring periodic cleaning
entries.cta: Get quotes for LSD Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: High-output turbo option for Premier and RS trims, delivering strong performance with generally solid reliability.
commonFailures: Cooling system component wear on high-mileage examples
Turbocharger bearing wear if oil change intervals are extended
entries.cta: Get quotes for LTG Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth and powerful V6 option for early Equinox, now less common in replacement enquiries due to lower UK sales.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on overheated units, particularly pre-2014
Water pump and thermostat housing leaks requiring cooling system attention
entries.cta: Get quotes for LFX Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Efficient turbo-diesel offered briefly in UK Equinox, delivering strong torque but requiring careful emissions system maintenance.
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey use causing limp mode and reduced performance
EGR valve carbon buildup leading to rough idle and emissions-related fault codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for LH7 Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Chevrolet Equinox Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chevrolet Equinox's most common engine issues centre on the 2.4L Ecotec (LEA/LAF) oil consumption and timing chain failures, plus turbo concerns on later 1.5L units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption - Why 2.4L Equinox Engines Burn Oil Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Equinox LS, LT, Premier (2010-2017, LEA & LAF 2.4L)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allows oil to enter combustion chamber, causing burning and increased consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Top-Up Management - Monitoring consumption between services
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil checks and top-ups; no mechanical intervention
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary measure only; does not fix root cause

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring Replacement - Addressing oil burn at source
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, piston ring replacement with upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Can restore oil control if caught before catastrophic wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage units; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Chevrolet Equinox worth £3,000-£8,000 with over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings offers better long-term value than repeated top-ups or partial repairs. We recommend getting multiple quotes before committing.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt LEA/LAF engine prices for your Chevrolet Equinox

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - Why Equinox 2.4L Engines Develop Rattling Noises
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Equinox LS, LT, Premier (2010-2017, LEA & LAF 2.4L)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing rattling and potential valve timing issues.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner Replacement - Addressing wear before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guides; chain inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before chain stretch causes damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattling is present or mileage exceeds 90k

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start, act immediately - a failed timing chain can destroy the engine. For Equinox models over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components is often more economical than chain replacement alone.
problems.cta: Get quotes for timing chain repair or replacement for your Chevrolet Equinox

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why 1.5L Equinox Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Equinox LT, Premier, RS (2018-2024, LYX & LSD 1.5L Turbo)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking or bearing wear causes boost irregularities and check engine light.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement - Addressing boost loss
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace turbocharger assembly with reconditioned unit
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine internals are healthy; verify compression first

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with turbo upgrade and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if turbo failure coincides with other wear items

problems.recommendation: If your 1.5T Equinox has over 70k miles and shows turbo symptoms, get a compression test first. If internals are worn, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers better long-term reliability than turbo-only replacement.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt LYX/LSD turbo engine prices for your Chevrolet Equinox

problems.h4: DPF & EGR Issues - Why Equinox Diesel Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Equinox LT, Premier, ACTIV (2018-2019, LH7 1.6L Diesel)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; EGR valve carbon buildup restricts airflow.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF Cleaning & EGR Service - Restoring emissions function
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF cleaning, EGR valve removal and decarbonising
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires regular motorway driving afterwards

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF/EGR Replacement - Addressing failed components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DPF and/or EGR valve with reconditioned units
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if cleaning fails; verify engine health first

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with updated emissions components
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if multiple emissions faults present on high-mileage unit

problems.recommendation: For diesel Equinox used mainly on short trips, regular motorway runs help prevent DPF issues. If limp mode persists after cleaning, compare rebuilt engine quotes - the LH7 is less common, so parts availability can affect repair costs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for LH7 diesel engine replacement for your Chevrolet Equinox

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chevrolet Equinox
finalCta.paragraph: The Equinox's most common engine problems - oil consumption, timing chain wear, turbo issues, and diesel emissions faults - can all be addressed cost-effectively through EngineMarket. Compare prices from trusted UK Chevrolet Equinox specialists, all offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chevrolet Equinox Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chevrolet Equinox and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Chevrolet Equinox 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L LH7 turbo-diesel was offered briefly in UK Equinox models from 2018-2019, delivering strong torque and efficiency but requiring careful emissions system maintenance on short-journey use.
commonFailurePoints: DPF clogging on short trips; EGR valve carbon buildup causing rough idle
items.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet Equinox 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Chevrolet Equinox 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern 1.5L turbo petrol (LYX/LSD) replaced the older 2.4L from 2018 onwards, offering improved efficiency and ULEZ compliance but introducing turbo-specific maintenance considerations.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking; carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Equinox 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Equinox 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo petrol (LTG) was the performance option for higher-spec Equinox models from 2018-2020, delivering strong power with generally solid reliability when maintained properly.
commonFailurePoints: Cooling system component wear on high-mileage examples; turbo bearing wear if oil changes delayed
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Equinox 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Equinox 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L naturally aspirated Ecotec (LEA/LAF) powered most early Equinox models in the UK, known for decent refinement but prone to oil consumption and timing chain issues at higher mileage.
commonFailurePoints: Excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear; timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Equinox 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Equinox 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L V6 (LFX) was the flagship petrol option for early Equinox models, offering smooth power but now less common in UK replacement enquiries due to lower original sales volume.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on overheated units; water pump
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Equinox 3.6L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Chevrolet Equinox Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Chevrolet Equinox production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Equinox launched in the UK with the 2.4L Ecotec petrol and optional 3.6L V6, establishing the model's initial engine lineup.
keyChanges: First-generation Equinox introduced to UK market
2.4L LEA/LAF Ecotec became the volume petrol engine
3.6L LFX V6 offered as performance option on higher trims
mainEngines: LAF - 2.4L petrol (early production LS / LT / Premier)
knownFor: 2.4L engines offer decent refinement but develop oil consumption from 60k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear becomes apparent on neglected units
3.6L V6 provides smooth power but cooling system attention needed
Early models (2010-2011) have highest incidence of oil consumption reports
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2013 Chevrolet Equinox replacement engines
years.ticker: Equinox 2.4L oil consumption fix • Equinox timing chain replacement • Equinox LEA engine price UK

years.description: Mid-cycle updates brought minor refinements, but the core 2.4L and 3.6L engines remained, with oil consumption issues now well-documented.
keyChanges: Minor facelift updates to styling and equipment
Engine lineup unchanged - LEA/LAF 2.4L and LFX 3.6L continued
Oil consumption and timing chain issues now widely reported by owners

years.preview: 2017 models still affected by LEA oil consumption; piston ring design unchanged
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2017 Chevrolet Equinox replacement engines
years.ticker: Equinox 2016 engine replacement • Equinox LEA piston rings • Equinox 2.4L rebuilt engine cost

years.description: All-new second generation introduced modern turbo petrol engines and a brief diesel option, marking a significant shift in Equinox powertrains.
keyChanges: Completely new platform and styling for second-generation Equinox
1.5L turbo LYX petrol replaced the 2.4L as the volume engine
2.0L turbo LTG offered on Premier and RS performance trims
1.6L LH7 diesel briefly available in UK market
mainEngines: LYX - 1.5L turbo petrol (LS / LT / Premier / RS / ACTIV)
knownFor: 1.5L LYX offers ULEZ compliance but introduces turbo-specific maintenance
Turbo wastegate actuator sticking reported on some LYX units from 50k miles
LH7 diesel requires regular motorway running to prevent DPF clogging
2.0L LTG generally reliable but cooling system attention needed on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Chevrolet Equinox replacement engines
years.ticker: Equinox 1.5 turbo replacement • Equinox LYX turbo fix • Equinox diesel DPF problem

years.description: Later second-generation models saw incremental updates to the 1.5L turbo and the introduction of the revised LSD engine code for 2023-2024.
keyChanges: LYX 1.5L turbo continued as the main petrol engine across most trims
LSD engine code introduced for 2023-2024 models with minor reliability revisions
Diesel option discontinued after 2019 in UK market
RS and ACTIV trims expanded the variant range
mainEngines: LYX - 1.5L turbo petrol (LS / LT / Premier / RS / ACTIV 2020-2022)
knownFor: LSD engine includes minor updates to turbo and fuel system components
Carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves remains a maintenance consideration
Turbo-related enquiries dominate replacement searches for this period
ULEZ compliance makes 1.5T engines popular for London and clean air zone owners
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Chevrolet Equinox replacement engines
years.ticker: Equinox 2022 engine replacement • Equinox LSD engine code • Equinox 1.5L turbo rebuild

faq.h2: Chevrolet Equinox Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Chevrolet Equinox engine prices range from £1,200 for a 2.4L petrol to £4,500 for a 3.6L V6, supply only. Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 labour depending on location.
keyPoints: 2.4L LEA/LAF rebuilt: £1,200-£2,400 supply only
1.5L turbo LYX rebuilt: £1,800-£3,200 supply only
1.6L diesel LH7 rebuilt: £2,200-£4,000 supply only
Labour for supply & fit typically £800-£1,500
All prices include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common Chevrolet Equinox engine problem?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption on the 2.4L LEA/LAF engines (2010-2017) is the single most-reported issue, caused by piston ring design allowing oil into the combustion chamber.
keyPoints: Affects LS, LT, Premier trims with 2.4L engines
Typically appears between 60,000-100,000 miles
Can lead to catalytic converter damage if ignored
Rebuilt engines with upgraded rings address the root cause
Regular oil checks essential on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for LEA/LAF engine replacement for your Equinox

items.question: How do I find my Chevrolet Equinox engine code?
items.answer: Your Equinox engine code is stamped on the engine block, listed in your V5C logbook under "D.2 Engine number", or can be instantly verified by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section D.2 for engine number prefix
Common codes: LEA, LAF (2.4L), LYX, LSD (1.5T), LH7 (1.6D)
Engine code also on VIN decoder tools and service records
Our DVLA lookup identifies code instantly from registration
Always confirm code before ordering a replacement engine
items.cta: Verify your Equinox engine code with our free tool

items.question: Are used Chevrolet Equinox engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Equinox engines can be reliable if sourced from reputable breakers with verified mileage and compression test results, but carry more uncertainty than rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Request compression test and service history before buying
Ensure engine code exactly matches your vehicle
Budget for potential ancillary part replacement during fitment
Used prices typically £800-£2,200 vs £1,200+ for rebuilt
Rebuilt engines include warranty; used units often have limited cover
items.cta: Check used Chevrolet Equinox engine availability today

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Equinox engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Equinox engines have worn parts replaced after inspection; rebuilt engines undergo complete strip-down with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: inspection, replace worn items, test - £1,200-£3,200
Rebuilt: full strip, machining, upgraded parts, dyno test - £1,200-£4,500
Rebuilt better addresses LEA oil consumption and timing chain issues
Both include minimum 12-month warranty through EngineMarket
Rebuilt offers better long-term value for high-mileage Equinox
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Equinox engine options

items.question: How long does a Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement takes 3-5 days for supply plus 1-2 days for fitting at a specialist garage, depending on engine availability and workshop schedule.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine dispatch: typically 3-5 working days
Garage fitting time: 1-2 days depending on complexity
Supply & fit packages can coordinate both for faster turnaround
Diesel and V6 engines may take longer due to lower stock levels
Express options sometimes available for common 2.4L and 1.5T codes
items.cta: Find Chevrolet Equinox engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Do you offer warranty on Chevrolet Equinox replacement engines?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Chevrolet Equinox engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the specialist supplier.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Some suppliers offer extended 24-month options at extra cost
Warranty requires professional fitting and service records
Covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet Equinox engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I get a Chevrolet Equinox engine supplied and fitted?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists offering full supply & fit service for Chevrolet Equinox engines, including installation, fluids, testing and warranty activation.
keyPoints: Nationwide network of Equinox-specialist garages available
Supply & fit labour typically adds £800-£1,500 to engine price
Professional fitting required to validate supplier warranty
Get bundled quotes for engine plus labour to compare total cost
Certified technicians ensure correct calibration and post-fit testing
items.cta: Book Chevrolet Equinox engine supply & fit service today

items.question: Is a Chevrolet Equinox engine replacement worth it?
items.answer: For a Chevrolet Equinox valued over £3,000 with otherwise good body and interior condition, a rebuilt engine at £1,200-£3,200 is often more economical than vehicle replacement.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost (£1,200-£4,500) vs vehicle market value
Factor in remaining life of transmission, suspension and bodywork
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can extend life by 50k+ miles
Consider ULEZ compliance if in London or clean air zones
Get multiple quotes to ensure best value for your specific Equinox
items.cta: Get expert advice on Equinox repair vs replace decision

items.question: What years of Chevrolet Equinox have engine problems?
items.answer: 2010-2017 Chevrolet Equinox models with 2.4L LEA/LAF engines have the highest incidence of oil consumption and timing chain issues; 2018+ 1.5T models have fewer but turbo-specific concerns.
keyPoints: 2010-2017 2.4L: oil consumption and timing chain most common
2018-2024 1.5T: turbo wastegate and carbon buildup issues
Diesel 2018-2019: DPF/EGR problems on short-journey use
Later LSD-coded 1.5T (2023-2024) includes minor reliability revisions
All problematic engines can be addressed with rebuilt replacements
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine prices for your Equinox model year
